betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Nuclear Spallation Scrap (Light Fission Waste)§f

§aLight Fission Waste is the scrap product of high-energy proton spallation.§f
The §bLight Fission Waste§f (`qmd:waste_fission:0`, ore dict `wasteFissionLight`) is a §cradioactive byproduct§f produced by §cspallation§f reactions in a QMD §bTarget Chamber§f — a high-energy proton beam is fired at a heavy metal target, breaking the target nucleus into lighter fission fragments and releasing neutrons. The chamber harvests the resulting §bLight Fission Waste§f as the item output.

§6Production Recipes (TargetChamberRecipes.java)§f:
Per `addQMDBalancedRecipe(...)`, several heavy targets yield Light Fission Waste when bombarded with high-energy protons:

| Target Item | Beam | Energy |
|-------------|------|--------|
| §bBismuth Dust§f (`dustBismuth`) | proton | §e≥ 100 MeV§f (line 248) |
| §bPlatinum Ingot§f (`ingotPlatinum`) | proton | §e≥ 200 MeV§f (line 257) |
| §bGold Ingot§f (`ingotGold`) | proton | §e≥ 200 MeV§f (line 260) |
| §bMercury fluid§f (`mercury` fluid) | proton | §e≥ 200 MeV§f (line 263) |
| §bMercury Ingot§f (`ingotMercury`) | proton | §e≥ 200 MeV§f (line 265) |
| §bLead Ingot§f (`ingotLead`) | proton | §e≥ 200 MeV§f (line 268) |
| §bTungsten Ingot§f (`ingotTungsten`) | proton | §e≥ 400 MeV§f (line 271) |

In all cases, the §boutput item§f is §bLight Fission Waste§f (`wasteFissionLight`), and the chamber releases §bneutron particles§f as byproducts. RF cost and cross-section vary per recipe.

§6Spallation vs. Fission§f:
- True §cnuclear fission§f is what happens inside a running §bFission Reactor§f — splitting U-235 / Pu-239 etc. with thermal neutrons.
- §cSpallation§f is what happens inside a §bTarget Chamber§f — a high-energy proton (100–400 MeV) chips fragments off a heavy nucleus.
- Both produce §bfission waste§f as a byproduct, which the §bNC Fuel Reprocessor§f can crack into useful isotopes.

§6Cleanup Recipe — Fuel Reprocessor (QMDRecipes.java:520)§f:
Per `NCRecipes.fuel_reprocessor.addRecipe("wasteFissionLight", ...)`:
- Input: 1 × §bLight Fission Waste§f
- Outputs (chanceOreStack with % chances):
  - §bStrontium Dust§f (1, 20% per 1 waste)
  - §bStrontium-90 Dust§f (1, 5%)
  - §bYttrium Dust§f (1, 5%)
  - §bZirconium Dust§f (1, 20%)
  - §bNiobium Dust§f (1, 5%)
  - §bMolybdenum Dust§f (1, 30%)
  - §bRuthenium-106 Dust§f (1, 5%)
  - §bSilver Dust§f (1, 10%)

§6Caesium-137 Radiation Source§f:
Per `QMDRadSources.java:43`, the Light Fission Waste ore dict is registered as a §bCaesium-137 radiation source§f — handle it in shielded containers (wearing a §bHBM Hazmat Suit§f or §bQMD HEV Armor§f).

§cWarning§f: Light Fission Waste is §cradioactive§f and §cwill poison§f players who carry it unprotected. Always store it in shielded §bHBM §f§eWaste Drum§f§c blocks and process it through the Fuel Reprocessor promptly.

§eTip§f: §bMercury fluid§f and §bBismuth Dust§f are the cheapest spallation targets. Use Bismuth if you want solid inputs, or pipe Mercury fluid directly into the §bTarget Chamber§f's fluid port.
